title: ESCALATE: Dockerfile runs as root — no USER directive

text: {"type":"escalate","finding":"Dockerfile runs as root","severity":"Critical","description":"server/Dockerfile has no USER directive — tmux-status-server process runs as root inside the container. Combined with default 0.0.0.0 bind, a Bottle vulnerability could give an attacker root-level access within the container.","options":[{"label":"Option 1: Add non-root user in Dockerfile","solution":"Add 'RUN useradd -r -s /usr/sbin/nologin appuser' then 'USER appuser' before ENTRYPOINT","pros":"Defense-in-depth, follows container security best practices, secure by default","cons":"Minor Dockerfile complexity increase"},{"label":"Option 2: Document --user flag for operators","solution":"Add HEALTHCHECK and document that container should be run with '--user' flag by operator","pros":"No Dockerfile change","cons":"Relies on operator discipline, less secure by default"}],"recommendation":"Option 1 — secure by default, standard practice","promotion_reason":"Natural FIX promoted to ESCALATE — max fix cycles (3) reached after 4 cycles"}
